name: Seated Calf Raise
resistance_profile: Bent-knee position biases the soleus; loaded through a full stretch.
rom_notes: Full stretch to full plantarflexion under the knee pad.
cues: Bent knee (~90 deg) targets the soleus
execution_steps: Sit at the machine with the balls of your feet on the platform and knees under the pad at about 90 degrees
Adjust the knee pad so it sits snugly on your lower thighs
Release the lock and lower your heels for a full stretch
Press up through the balls of your feet to full plantarflexion
Pause at the bottom, then lift and lower slowly
good_when: You want to bias the soleus with the bent-knee position
Adding calf volume that fatigues the knee-flexed muscle differently than standing work
Seated stability lets you focus on a full, controlled range
bad_when: You want to emphasize the gastrocnemius (use a straight-leg standing or leg-press raise)
No seated calf machine is available
